cashback.notes: Requires activating the ₹299/90-day 'Grocery Cashback' pack in the AU 0101 app; cashback capped at ₹500 per 30-day period
Requires activating the ₹199/90-day 'Apparel Cashback' pack; cashback capped at ₹500 per 30-day period

loungeAccess.conditions: Requires activating the ₹399/90-day lounge pack in the AU 0101 app; capped at 2 domestic airport lounge visits per 90-day period

milestoneBenefits.reward: Additional 5% cashback up to ₹500 (requires the ₹299/90-day milestone pack)
Additional 2% cashback up to ₹1,000 (requires the ₹199/90-day milestone pack)

faqTemplateAnswers.Is this card lifetime free?: AU markets it as lifetime free with no mandatory joining or annual fee, but most meaningful benefits (accelerated cashback, higher reward multipliers, lounge access) require activating optional 90-day 'packs' priced ₹49–₹399 each via the AU 0101 app, so it isn't free if you actually want to use its features.
faqTemplateAnswers.Is it worth it?: Worth it only if you actively manage the AU 0101 app every 90 days to activate the specific packs matching your spending — otherwise you're either paying quarterly fees for unused benefits or getting minimal base value. Casual users may find a simpler flat-rate card better value.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should get this card?: Engaged, app-savvy users who want to customize their card's cashback, rewards and lounge benefits every quarter based on changing spending needs.
faqTemplateAnswers.Does it support UPI?: AU Bank's app-first cards generally support UPI linking via the AU 0101 app; confirm current UPI availability for this specific Visa variant directly with AU Bank.
faqTemplateAnswers.Can I get lounge access with this card?: Yes, but only if you activate the ₹399/90-day lounge pack, which gives up to 2 complimentary domestic airport lounge visits per quarter.
faqTemplateAnswers.What income is required to qualify?: AU Bank does not publish a fixed minimum income for the LIT card; eligibility depends on internal credit assessment, with applicants generally required to be 21–65 years old (self-employed) or 21–60 (salaried).
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should avoid this card?: Anyone who wants a simple, predictable, genuinely free card without recurring micro-fees should avoid LIT — its opt-in pricing can end up costing more than a flat-fee card if benefits aren't actively managed every quarter.
faqTemplateAnswers.What are the best alternatives?: HDFC Pixel Play, HDFC Millennia, or Equitas Selfe Credit Card for simpler flat-benefit lifetime-free cards.
